title: KeyFlux
description: Extend Unreal Engine's Sequencer with tools to export and import transform data directly from the editor. Capture an actor's keyframes as CSV and reapply them to any actor in a Level Sequence with frame-accurate precision.

sections.title: Overview
body: KeyFlux is a Sequencer companion plugin for Unreal Engine that empowers artists and technical directors to seamlessly export and import transform keyframes between Unreal and external tools such as Maya, Blender, or Excel. It extends Sequencer's functionality with data-driven workflows, enabling frame-accurate roundtripping of motion data.
All features are implemented in C++ as an editor-only utility - no runtime dependencies, no Blueprint classes.

sections.title: Core Features
body: CSV Export - Export actor transform keyframes (Location, Rotation, Scale) to a CSV file from any open Level Sequence.
CSV Import - Import transform keyframes from a CSV back into the Sequencer with automatic actor binding and track creation if missing.
Frame-Accurate Mapping - Uses the sequence's Display Rate and Tick Resolution to ensure perfect time alignment.
Intelligent File Naming - Every export is automatically named: <SequenceName>_<ActorName>_<Time>_<Date>.csv.
Editor-Only - No runtime dependencies; not included in packaged builds.

sections.title: Installation
steps: Copy the KeyFlux folder into your project's Plugins/ directory: <YourProject>/Plugins/KeyFlux/
Rebuild your project from Visual Studio or Rider.
Launch Unreal Engine and go to Edit → Plugins, search for KeyFlux, and enable it.

sections.title: Exporting Transform Data
steps: Select one or more actors in the level.
Click Export Transform Data from the KeyFlux tab or toolbar.
The plugin creates a CSV file in <ProjectRoot>/Saved/SequencerExports/ containing all transform keyframes for those actors.

sections.caption: KeyFlux Export and Import buttons in the Sequencer toolbar

sections.title: Importing Transform Data
steps: Select the actor you want to apply the imported motion to.
Click Import Transform Data in the KeyFlux tab.
Choose the CSV file to import.
The plugin recreates transform keyframes on that actor's transform track, aligned with the correct frame rate.

sections.title: CSV Format
sections.body: Column order: Frame, Time, PosX, PosY, PosZ, RotX, RotY, RotZ, ScaleX, ScaleY, ScaleZ. Files are saved to Saved/SequencerExports/ and named automatically: <SequenceName>_<ActorName>_<Time>_<Date>.csv. Example: Shot03_CameraActor_19-32-58_2025-11-10.csv. Compatible with Grapher for trajectory visualization.

sections.title: Technical Details
body: Module Type - Editor module only; not included in packaged builds.
C++ Classes - 5 classes: Module, Commands, Style, Exporter, Importer.
Blueprint Nodes - None (all features implemented in C++).

sections.title: FAQ
body: Can I import data to a different actor? Yes. Select any actor in the level before importing and KeyFlux will apply the transform track to that actor.
Does it handle frame rates correctly? Yes. The plugin uses the Level Sequence's Display Rate and Tick Resolution to ensure perfect time alignment.
What data is included? Frame, Time, Position (X/Y/Z), Rotation (X/Y/Z), Scale (X/Y/Z).

sections.title: Known Limitations
sections.body: Currently supports only transform data (Location, Rotation, Scale). Does not yet handle custom properties (FOV, Light Intensity, Visibility, etc.). CSV import assumes the same frame rate as the source sequence.
